violations.finding: Pre-existing CJK text in BLOCKED error message: '必须使用正常的UI流程创建session，永远不允许使用代码创建session！'
violations.recommendation: Translate error message to English. Attribution note: per task 20260509-153155 exemption, if this is a verbatim user-binding quote it should be preserved in docs/dev/ticket-*.md only; the stderr diagnostic string must be English with task-id citation.

violations.finding: Pre-existing CJK text in comment: verbatim user prompt '全部commit push' cited in block comment
violations.recommendation: Move verbatim Chinese user-binding quote to docs/dev/ticket-*.md (already outside Standard 6 scope). Replace comment reference with English description plus task-id citation per Standard 6 exemption contract.

standards_passed: no-inline-code (standard-1): no new inline executable code introduced in changed files
no-hardcoded-values (standard-2): no new hardcoded env-specific values introduced; TTL constants are named policy constants
use-source-venv (standard-3): all new python invocations in commit.md use source venv/bin/activate; pre-existing bare python3 in push.md and test_final_sweep.sh not introduced by this diff
integer-step-numbering (standard-4): no decimal step numbering found in any changed file
meaningful-naming (standard-5): no forbidden naming patterns in filenames
english-only (standard-6): no CJK introduced in changed files; unconditional scan found pre-existing violations in hooks/ (see violations list with introduced_in_diff=false)
merge-similar-scripts (standard-7): write-commit-grant.py and write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py serve distinct purposes (single-use grant vs multi-use sentinel); no merge appropriate
concise-documentation (standard-8): all documentation changes are functional precision fixes; no marketing language or verbosity introduced
parameterized-scripts (standard-9): no new unparameterized values; TTL literals 30/600 are named constants accepted by project precedent

dangling-references (standard-10): all referenced scripts (write-commit-grant.py, write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py, execute-push.py, resolve-dev-report.py, resolve-close-report.sh, step7-spec-update.py) exist on disk
merge-conflicts (standard-11): no merge conflict markers found in any changed file

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed no newly-introduced blocking violations. Raised 4 issues: (1) 'ZERO violations' phrasing ambiguity; (2) Standard 6 pre-existing CJK in hooks not in changed-files scope; (3) Standard 3 pre-existing bare python3 in push.md/test_final_sweep.sh; (4) Standard 1 pre-existing executable bash blocks in command/agent .md files. One OBSERVATION_ONLY on TTL constant naming.

findings.issue: ZERO violations phrasing is only defensible as zero newly-introduced blocking violations; pre-existing style debt exists
findings.proposed_fix: Change summary to: 'No newly introduced blocking violations were found in the diff; advisory pre-existing findings are noted below.'
findings.rationale: Report now distinguishes introduced_in_diff=false for pre-existing findings and uses violations_introduced_in_diff=0 in summary

findings.issue: Standard 6 unconditional scan found pre-existing CJK in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:1271 and hooks/pretool-git-privilege-guard.py:11 not covered by mascot-exemption
findings.proposed_fix: Classify as advisory_pre_existing with introduced_in_diff: false; include in violations list for completeness
findings.rationale: Both CJK findings are verified. They are pre-existing (not introduced by this diff). Both appear in files within Standard 6 scope (hooks/*.sh, hooks/*.py). Reported with introduced_in_diff=false. Per spec, close.md applies AC-2.6(b): overlap-only is advisory; these are not in diff-changed lines so they do not gate this cycle's CLOSE verdict.

findings.issue: Standard 3: pre-existing bare python3 in commands/push.md:185/193 and hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh lacks venv activation
findings.proposed_fix: State: new commit.md invocations are venv-prefixed and compliant; bare python3 in push.md and test_final_sweep.sh is pre-existing debt not introduced by this diff
findings.rationale: push.md documents the intentional stdlib-only rationale at lines 211-213; test_final_sweep.sh is a test harness that runs system python3. Both pre-date this diff. Not reported as violations because introduced_in_diff=false and the --changed-files scoping excludes pre-existing debt from cycle gating.

findings.issue: Standard 1: changed lines in commands/commit.md touch pre-existing executable bash code blocks; agents/push-analyst.md modifies a Python code block
findings.proposed_fix: Reword to: 'No newly introduced Standard 1 blocker under diff gating. Touched blocks are pre-existing command/agent directive snippets.'
findings.rationale: The project's established convention uses code blocks in command/agent .md files as directive specifications for subagents — not inline implementations. The changed lines are corrections within pre-existing code blocks (python -> python3, TTL values), not new inline implementations. No new Standard 1 violation introduced.

findings.issue: Standard 2/9: changed TTL literals 10->30 and 180->600 are named constants but could be considered hardcoded
findings.proposed_fix: TTL literals are named policy/test constants (GRANT_TTL_MINUTES, PUSH_ANALYST_GRANT_TTL_SECONDS) accepted by existing project precedent, not unparameterized environment-specific values
findings.rationale: Named constants that never vary between environments are explicitly excluded from Standard 2 per the inspector spec. TTL values are policy decisions, not environment-specific paths or identifiers.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Two pre-existing Standard 6 violations in hooks/ files added to report with introduced_in_diff=false. Summary field now includes violations_introduced_in_diff=0 and violations_pre_existing=2 to clarify cycle gating impact.
